briefdescription.para: The static initializer for struct membuf.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a memory buffer.

a pointer to the memory region to be used by the buffer. If not NULL, and LELY_NO_MALLOC is not defined, the buffer takes ownership of the region at ptr and MAY reallocate or free the region during subsequent calls to membuf_reserve() and membuf_fini().
the number of bytes available at ptr.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the first byte in a memory buffer.
Returns the total number of bytes written to a memory buffer.
Returns the number of unused bytes remaining in a memory buffer.
Resizes a memory buffer, if necessary, to make room for at least an additional size bytes.

detaileddescription.para: This function may also shrink the buffer if it is larger than required. Although this functions preserves the data already written to the buffer, it invalidates the pointers obtained from previous calls to membuf_alloc().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a memory buffer.
the required capacity of the buffer.

simplesect.para: the new capacity of the buffer, or 0 on error (or if both the current capacity and size are 0). The new capacity can be larger than the requested capacity.

briefdescription.para: Adjusts the position indicator of a memory buffer by offset bytes.

detaileddescription.para: The offset will be truncated to a valid range: if negative, -offset must not exceed membuf_size(); if positive, offset must not exceed membuf_capacity().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a memory buffer.
the offset (in bytes) with respect to the current position indicator.

briefdescription.para: Creates region of *size bytes in a memory buffer, starting at the current position indicator given by membuf_size(), and sets the indicator to the end of the requested region.

detaileddescription.para: If the requested size is larger than the current capacity, *size will be updated with the actual size of the region. If the requested size turns out to be too large or too small, the region can be adjusted with membuf_seek().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a memory buffer.
the address of the value containing the requested size. On exit, *size is updated with the actual size.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a memory region of at least *size consecutive bytes (which may be 0). Note that this pointer is only valid until the next call to membuf_reserve().

briefdescription.para: Writes data to a memory buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Writing starts at the current position indicator given by membuf_size().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a memory buffer.
a pointer to the data to be copied.
the number of bytes to be written.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written, which may be smaller than size in case of insufficient capacity.

briefdescription.para: Flushes size bytes from the beginning of a memory buffer.
This header file is part of the utilities library; it contains the memory buffer declarations.

detaileddescription.para:
